name: Senegal
headline: Senegal CONFIRMATION at 33.6 (working baseline applied May 14) — no new material evidence today. Asylum-seeker displacement to Cote d'Ivoire, Benin, Togo, Gambia is documented in continuing reporting (TheNewHumanitarian May 5, MambaOnline May 2) but the underlying enforcement evidence (70+ detained, 24 HIV charges, 25.6% treatment-attendance drop) was the basis for the May 9/May 14 cycle and would constitute double-counting if rescored here. The displacement signal is a PRO-dimension forward signal but the scale is not yet quantified — assessor judgment is that quantified displacement scale would warrant further movement; absent quantification, hold at 33.6. Per scanner skip note: 'DO NOT re-propose 37.5 → 33.6 (applied May 14)' — this proposal confirms 33.6 with no further movement. Forward signals documented; no score change.
workingBaseline_note: Working baseline 33.6 is the May 14 applied score (index-file canonical). Rotation-state shows 37.5 — pre-May-14-application. This proposal confirms 33.6, the post-May-14 canonical state.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. May 14 baseline EMP -0.5 reflected medically-incorrect HIV transmission charges and treatment-attendance harm; no further EMP-dimension evidence today.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. May 14 baseline ACT -0.5 reflected state response as enforcement rather than mitigation; no further ACT-dimension evidence today.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. May 14 baseline EQU -0.25 reflected compound differential-harm pattern; no further EQU-dimension evidence today.

BND.rationale: Sustained. Asylum-seeker displacement to neighboring states is a BND-dimension forward signal (forced departure = boundary-of-protection failure at national scale), but scale is not yet quantified — holding pending quantification.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. May 14 baseline ACC -0.25 reflected state enforcement of medically-incorrect criminal charges; no further ACC-dimension evidence today.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. May 14 baseline SYS sustained at 3.0.

INT.rationale: Sustained. Mixed-democracy posture; UNAIDS engagement continues.

evidence.summary: TheNewHumanitarian May 5 / MambaOnline May 2: asylum-seekers fleeing Senegal to Cote d'Ivoire, Benin, Togo, Gambia due to Article 319 enforcement risk. Scale not yet quantified.

May 14 baseline (33.6) anchored in: 70+ arrests under Article 319 since February; 24 charged with 'voluntary HIV transmission' (including undetectable viral-load cases); 25.6% HIV treatment center attendance drop in one month; first 6-year conviction April 10.
UNAIDS March 18 2026 press release condemning Senegal Article 319 law and HIV response impact.

keyEvidence: No new material evidence dated May 14-15 — confirmation cycle.
Asylum-seeker displacement to four neighboring states (Cote d'Ivoire, Benin, Togo, Gambia) is BND-dimension forward signal — scale not yet quantified.
May 14 baseline (33.6) anchored in compound enforcement evidence: 70+ arrests, 24 HIV charges (including undetectable cases), 25.6% treatment drop, first 6-year conviction.
Per scanner skip note: do not re-propose 37.5 → 33.6 (applied May 14).
Confirmation cycle; forward signals documented; no score change.
rationale: Confirmation at 33.6 / Developing. No new material evidence dated May 14-15 to warrant further movement. The asylum-seeker displacement signal is real and documented in continuing reporting but lacks quantified scale — assessor judgment is that quantified scale would warrant further BND-dimension movement; absent quantification, hold. Per scanner skip note, this proposal explicitly does NOT re-propose the May 14 working baseline application. Math: (3+2+2.5+1.25+2.5+1.75+3+2.5)/8 = 18.5/8 = 2.3125 → ((2.3125-1)/4)*100 = 32.8125 → 32.8. MATH-HYGIENE FLAG: applied score 33.6 differs from dimensional arithmetic 32.8 by 0.8pt (above the 0.5pt drift threshold). Documenting for audit; this is the May 14 applied state, not introduced by this proposal.
watches: Asylum-seeker displacement scale quantification (UNHCR or NGO data)
HIV treatment attendance trajectory (after May 25.6% drop)
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: 'forced-departure-as-protection-failure-at-national-scale' is a BND-dimension forward signal that requires quantification before scoring. Methodology should establish a quantification threshold (e.g., 1,000+ documented departures, or %-of-affected-population) above which the signal triggers automatic BND-dimension movement.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Composite 33.6 sits in central-lower Developing band (21-40), 12.6pt above 20/21 boundary and 6.4pt below 40/41 boundary.

math_check.math_hygiene_flag: 0.8pt drift between dimensional arithmetic (32.8) and applied score (33.6). Above 0.5pt audit threshold. This drift was introduced by the May 14 application; not introduced by this confirmation proposal. Documented for audit. Recommendation: index file should be adjusted to 32.8 OR proposed dimensions adjusted to produce 33.6 cleanly.
math_check.validator_check: Math confirmation cycle. Pre-existing 0.8pt drift flagged for audit.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
